title: Inclusive Vehicle Designs, Centro Transit Integration, and Intoxication/Helmet Safety Tools
summary: Veo presents two new vehicle types: three-wheeled trike for riders with mobility limitations (storage capacity for oxygen tanks, walkers, larger bags) and cargo bike targeting food desert areas (zip code 13205 identified). Centro transit integration announced: riders connecting to any Centro bus stop via QR code scan receive $1 rebate -- no cost to Centro or city. Discussion of 44 existing Veo bike racks citywide (25 pre-existing, rest converted from prior bike share program), with council requesting more at bus stops. Nave raises intoxication concerns; Veo developing cognition test tool for spring 2026 to increase friction for impaired riders at targeted times. AI-powered helmet detection tool offers rebates as incentive (carrot approach) since NY state law does not require helmets for riders 18+. Equity concerns raised: helmet mandates and ID requirements could become access barriers. Council presses on minors accessing service despite 18+ terms of service -- transitions to extended discussion in next segment.

political_signal.description: Council pressing on minor rider safety, intoxication risks, and helmet enforcement gaps

processing_notes: Law Department in detected_departments is from 'legalistic' terminology in helmet law discussion; excluded as not a substantive department reference
$130K and $1 amounts repeated from prior segments -- same opportunity zone data
Centro = Central New York Regional Transportation Authority (public transit); not in entity registry
Zip code 13205 identified for cargo bike food desert deployment
Segment 3 of 6 from Public Transportation Committee Meeting, Tuesday, February 4th, 2026
Transcript corrections applied: SU -> Syracuse University
